name: BEN JOHN VILLABESA
title: Full-Stack Software Engineer // Building the Future

bio: Full-stack software engineer with 5+ years of experience building end-to-end systems — from warehouse management and supply chain platforms to AI-powered content orchestration and cybersecurity intelligence tools.

Currently building AI-integrated platforms at PrimeDesk Solutions and Cecuri, working with self-hosted LLM inference (vLLM, Ollama), agentic AI pipelines (LangChain/LangGraph), and multi-GPU task orchestration.
I enjoy owning the full stack — designing backend APIs, building responsive frontends, developing mobile apps, and wiring up the infrastructure to make it all run reliably in production.

projects.title: ShortsEngine
projects.description: AI-powered YouTube Shorts automation platform that generates and uploads vertical video content at scale — combining multi-GPU inference (Ollama LLM, Stable Diffusion, Coqui TTS), trending topic discovery, video clipping automation with Whisper transcription and LLM highlight detection, and YouTube API integration for scheduled publishing and analytics.
longDescription: Built a full-stack content automation platform that generates YouTube Shorts from trending topics end-to-end — from news discovery and article scraping through AI script generation, image synthesis, text-to-speech with word-level timestamps, video assembly with animated captions, and automatic YouTube upload. The system runs on a 4x RTX 4090 server with GPU-specific Celery task queues: GPUs 0-2 for parallel Stable Diffusion image generation via ComfyUI WebSocket API, GPU 3 for Coqui XTTS-v2 voice cloning, and Ollama for script generation.

The video clipping pipeline automates repurposing of trending content from YouTube, Twitch, and Twitter — downloading videos via yt-dlp, transcribing with Whisper for word-level timestamps, detecting viral-worthy moments using LLM analysis with customizable prompts (e.g., rap battle punchlines, crowd reactions), extracting clips with FFmpeg, applying 9:16 vertical cropping, and queuing for approval or auto-upload. Each account can configure custom highlight detection prompts, clip duration constraints, crop modes, and auto-approval settings.

The platform supports multi-account management with per-account configuration for niche, prompt templates, visual styles, TTS voices, caption styles, scheduling intervals, and YouTube OAuth credentials. The React admin dashboard provides account management, content queue with preview and approval workflow, real-time generation progress tracking via Redis pub/sub, clipping source management, and YouTube analytics sync. Infrastructure includes PostgreSQL for persistence, Redis for Celery broker and progress tracking, MinIO for media storage, and Nginx reverse proxy with SSL.
features: Multi-GPU content pipeline with task queue routing: image generation (GPUs 0-2), TTS (GPU 3), LLM script generation (Ollama)
End-to-end Shorts generation: topic discovery → article scraping → script generation → image synthesis → TTS → video assembly → YouTube upload
Video clipping automation: download trending videos (YouTube/Twitch/Twitter) → Whisper transcription → LLM highlight detection → clip extraction → vertical crop
Customizable highlight detection with per-account LLM prompts (e.g., rap battle punchlines, crowd reactions, controversy)
Stable Diffusion image generation via ComfyUI WebSocket API with Ken Burns animation effects
Coqui XTTS-v2 voice cloning with word-level timestamps for animated captions
YouTube OAuth integration with automatic upload, scheduling, and analytics sync
Multi-account management with per-account niche, prompts, visual styles, TTS voices, and scheduling
Content queue with video preview, approval workflow, and bulk operations
Real-time generation progress tracking via Redis with stage-by-stage status updates
Automatic retry with topic fallback when article scraping or script generation fails
Scheduled content generation with configurable intervals and auto-approval options
Clipping source management for YouTube channels, playlists, and individual videos
Video processing with 9:16 vertical cropping, caption overlay, and thumbnail generation

projects.title: MODEXIS
projects.description: AI-powered content orchestration platform for SEO-driven web publishing at scale — combining LLM-based article generation, geographic/vertical directory graph targeting, multi-channel publishing (Astro SSR + WordPress), campaign lifecycle management with domain provisioning, and enterprise content governance through playbooks, QA agents, and policy enforcement.
longDescription: Built a full-stack content orchestration platform that automates the entire lifecycle of SEO-optimized web content — from topic research and outline generation through AI-powered article writing, quality assurance, deduplication, and multi-channel publishing. The system runs a local vLLM inference server (Llama-3.1-8B on dual GPUs) with micro-batching for throughput, orchestrated by Celery workers across specialized task queues for bulk dispatch, per-site generation, and publishing.

The platform models a directory graph of verticals (industries), geographic markets (state/city/neighborhood), page types (hub, money, supporting), and topic intents (informational, transactional, navigational) to produce targeted content at scale. Campaigns coordinate multi-site generation with automatic domain provisioning (DNS validation, Let's Encrypt SSL, Nginx config), while playbooks define reusable content workflows with routing rules, page matrix compilation, and validation gates. A policy system enforces content contracts including word count constraints, required sections (P3 template), deduplication thresholds, internal link mesh rules, source restrictions, and vertical-specific disclaimers.

The admin Control Center provides a 3-step article generation wizard, campaign management with lifecycle tracking and per-entry status monitoring, playbook configuration, job monitoring with real-time progress, and a knowledge base for content collections and entity management. The public-facing Astro SSR site dynamically resolves page specs via host-header routing for campaign domains, renders directory pages with JSON-LD schema markup, and generates per-domain XML sitemaps. Monitoring is handled through Prometheus metrics scraping and Grafana dashboards tracking LLM throughput, job status, and system health.
features: Local LLM inference via vLLM (Llama-3.1-8B, dual GPU) with micro-batching for high-throughput article generation
End-to-end content pipeline: topic generation → outline building → article writing → QA → dedup → publishing
Directory graph architecture with verticals, geographic markets, page types, and topic intent classification
Campaign lifecycle management with multi-site orchestration, per-entry status tracking, and bulk article scoping
Automatic domain provisioning with DNS validation, Let's Encrypt SSL, and Nginx configuration management
Multi-channel publishing to Astro SSR (host-header routed campaign sites) and WordPress REST API
Playbook system for reusable content workflows with routing rules, page matrix compilation, and validation gates
Enterprise content governance through policy profiles (word count contracts, P3 template enforcement, source restrictions, dedup thresholds)
3-step content generation wizard with campaign-scoped bulk generation and real-time job monitoring
QA agent with fact-checking, confidence scoring, and P3 template compliance with auto-rewrite capability
Internal link mesh generation with link policy enforcement and URL inventory tracking
Dynamic XML sitemap generation, JSON-LD schema markup, and GSC/IndexNow indexing submission
Prometheus + Grafana observability stack monitoring LLM throughput, job status, and system health
Comprehensive E2E test suite (104 Playwright tests) covering CRUD, campaign lifecycle, and content workflows

projects.title: CERBERUS AI
projects.description: Full-stack cybersecurity intelligence and AI-powered document analysis platform with multi-LLM integration, automated threat research pipelines, local file management with RAG-based Q&A, and scheduled email reporting.
longDescription: Built a comprehensive cybersecurity intelligence platform that combines AI-powered document processing, threat research, and investigation capabilities into a unified web application. The system integrates multiple LLM backends (Ollama, Google Gemini) via LangChain for flexible AI orchestration, supporting PDF analysis with OCR fallback, conversational chat, and structured incident investigations.

The research pipeline features an API-free discovery engine using RSS feeds, XML sitemaps, and polite domain crawling — with optional SearXNG, SERPAPI, and Tavily integration. Research jobs follow a draft-first workflow with multi-criteria scoring, AU-bias filtering, keyword pattern matching, and real-time progress streaming via Server-Sent Events. Completed research can be finalized into structured Markdown reports and delivered on schedule via configurable SMTP email automation.

A local storage module provides hierarchical file management supporting PDF, DOCX, CSV, XLSX, and MSG formats with batch OCR processing and multi-turn RAG-powered chat sessions over uploaded documents. The platform also includes ISO 27001 policy review with per-policy alignment scoring, an immutable cache management layer for discovery results, and full Docker Compose orchestration with PostgreSQL, SearXNG, Nginx reverse proxy, and health-checked services.
features: Multi-LLM integration with dynamic model selection (Ollama + Gemini via LangChain)
PDF processing with text extraction and Tesseract OCR fallback for scanned documents
Automated threat research pipeline with draft-first workflow and multi-criteria scoring
API-free discovery engine using RSS feeds, XML sitemaps, and polite domain crawling
Real-time research progress streaming via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
Local file management with hierarchical folders and batch OCR processing
RAG-powered multi-turn chat sessions over uploaded documents (PDF, DOCX, CSV, XLSX, MSG)
Scheduled email automation with SMTP config, recipient groups, and delivery logging
ISO 27001 policy review with per-policy alignment scoring and rating methodology
Quick AI-driven incident investigations with structured summaries and references
In-memory discovery cache with TTL, domain-level eviction, and refetch capabilities
Dockerized deployment with PostgreSQL, SearXNG, Nginx reverse proxy, and health checks

projects.title: WAREHOUSE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
projects.description: Full-stack multi-warehouse inventory and supply chain platform with mobile warehouse operations, barcode scanning, point-of-sale, and real-time stock tracking across warehouses and retail stores.
longDescription: Built a comprehensive Warehouse Management System that tracks inventory across the entire supply chain — from supplier goods receipt at warehouses, through internal transfers and deliveries, to retail store point-of-sale. The platform supports multi-warehouse and multi-store operations with role-based access control for admins, managers, warehouse staff, and viewers.

The system features a robust transaction workflow engine supporting four transaction types (Receive, Transfer, Deliver, Pull Out) each with strict status state machines. Delivery transactions follow an extended 8-stage workflow (draft → picking → DR generated → checking → packing → dispatched → received) with mobile pick/check operations using barcode scanning.

A companion mobile app built with Expo serves dual roles: warehouse staff use it for picking, checking, and dispatching deliveries with barcode scanning, while store staff use it as a full point-of-sale terminal with cart management, multiple payment methods, and receipt printing. Every action across the system is recorded in an immutable audit log with before/after JSON snapshots for complete traceability.
features: Multi-warehouse real-time inventory tracking with reserved/available quantities
4-type transaction workflow engine with strict status state machines
Mobile barcode scanning for warehouse pick/check/dispatch operations
Mobile point-of-sale with cart, multiple payment methods, and receipt printing
Store inventory auto-populated on delivery receipt via process_delivery_to_store
Drag-and-drop delivery receipt print layout designer
Immutable audit trail logging every create, update, delete, and status change
Role-based access control (Admin, Manager, Warehouse Staff, Viewer)
Product catalog with SKU auto-generation, barcode lookup, and lot/batch tracking
Dashboard with KPIs, supplier management, and customer management
